summary:
Blood glucose estimation is critical in managing conditions like gestational diabetes and detecting fetal growth abnormalities such as large-for-date infants, both of which stem from impaired glucose metabolism. In contrast, Rh incompatibility leads to hemolysis through immunological mechanisms unrelated to glycaemic status, making blood sugar assessment irrelevant in this context.  

\boxed{D}
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 1 summarized): Blood glucose estimation is critical in managing conditions like gestational diabetes and detecting fetal growth abnormalities such as large-for-date infants, both of which stem from impaired glucose metabolism. In contrast, Rh incompatibility leads to hemolysis through immunological mechanisms unrelated to glycaemic status, making blood sugar assessment irrelevant in this context.  

\boxed{D}. Answer choice: D
